meta.name: Sentinel — Wiki golden set (docs/sentinel_demo_patients_50), app schema
meta.note: records from Wiki; expected computed by core

encounters.summary: Bloods and urine ACR checked; medications reviewed.

wiki_expected.trajectory: gradual decline ~3.7 mL/min/year over 3 years (sub-rapid, no GFR-category crossing); sustained severe albuminuria (A3)

surface.finding: SGLT2 inhibitor indicated and not prescribed
RAS inhibitor indicated and not prescribed

proposed_actions.detail: Initiate RAS inhibitor (ACEi or ARB), titrate to maximum tolerated
proposed_actions.trigger_threshold: albuminuria A3 without diabetes (strong, 1B), currently on amlodipine only

proposed_actions.detail: U&E (creatinine, potassium) at RAS inhibitor initiation and repeat within 2-4 weeks
proposed_actions.trigger_threshold: RAS inhibitor initiation monitoring (within 2-4 weeks; sooner given eGFR 33)
significant albuminuria (A3, ACR 380 mg/g >=300) and very-high-risk tier
proposed_actions.draft_text: Re: 58M. Referral for very-high-risk CKD (G3b A3): eGFR 33, ACR 380 mg/g (severely increased albuminuria), non-diabetic, with a gradual eGFR decline over 3 years. Not currently on RAS inhibitor or SGLT2 inhibitor; both proposed. Grateful for specialist assessment and management.

surface.summary: Albuminuric CKD (A3, non-diabetic) — RAS inhibitor indicated, not prescribed.
Albuminuric CKD (ACR 380 >= 200), eGFR 33 (>= 20) — SGLT2 inhibitor indicated, not prescribed.
Draft nephrology referral — trigger: severe albuminuria (ACR 380 >= 300 mg/g, A3); KFRE 5-yr 8.4% (>= 3%). (KFRE 5-yr 8.4%.)

suppress.reason: Slope ~3.7 mL/min/yr — below rapid threshold (5.0). Stable.
Non-diabetic — nsMRA is a T2D-gated indication; do NOT surface.
Already on a statin.

encounters.summary: Bloods and urine ACR checked; medications reviewed.

surface.finding: Creatinine-based eGFR likely overestimates true GFR due to low muscle mass; confirm before staging-driven decisions
surface.trigger_threshold: eGFR reliability rule at extremes of muscle mass

proposed_actions.trigger_threshold: creatinine-based eGFR unreliable in low muscle mass

proposed_actions.detail: Hold staging-driven treatment and referral decisions (including any RASi decision) pending cystatin C result
proposed_actions.trigger_threshold: true GFR uncertain; avoid acting on potentially falsely reassuring eGFR

surface.summary: CKD, age 72 — statin indicated for CV-risk reduction, not prescribed.

suppress.reason: Low muscle mass — creatinine-based eGFR overestimates true GFR. Confirm with cystatin C.
Indicated, but eGFR unreliable (muscle mass) — confirm with cystatin C before acting.
ACR 45 (< 200), non-diabetic, eGFR 52 > 45, no HF — not indicated.
Non-diabetic — nsMRA is a T2D-gated indication; do NOT surface.
No referral criterion met (KFRE 0.2%) — do not escalate.

patients.name: James R.
problems: current trimethoprim course for urinary tract infection

encounters.summary: Prescribed trimethoprim — may transiently raise creatinine.
Bloods and urine ACR checked; medications reviewed.

wiki_expected.trajectory: stable at baseline; single transient dip during antibiotic course

suppress.reason: non-steady-state creatinine pseudo-rise from trimethoprim; not true GFR loss; not progression; remains G2, not CKD
suppress.trigger_threshold: suppression rule: trimethoprim blocks tubular secretion of creatinine

proposed_actions.detail: Routine repeat U&E after trimethoprim course completed to confirm return to baseline

suppress.reason: G2 A1 with no damage marker — does not meet the CKD definition gate.
